a260713p1.summary: Staring down the barrel of policy action that could make open models a permanent second class citizen. — ∙ Paid
a260713p1.title: Open weight AI models are facing an existential policy test in the US, with Anthropic leading a campaign against Chinese models over distillation concerns (Nathan Lambert/Interconnects AI)

a260713p10.summary: Brussels will propose gradual access for different ages in response to concerns over child safety online
a260713p10.title: European Commission President Ursula von der Leyen says after the summer the bloc is set to propose a "social media start date for minors" with "gradual access" (Barbara Moens/Financial Times)

a260713p11.summary: Operating out of a Tokyo high-rise, a military intelligence unit finds the high-tech equipment that Russia needs to wage war.
a260713p11.title: Investigation: Western intelligence officials identify a Russian military intelligence unit in Tokyo that smuggles high-tech components for the war in Ukraine (New York Times)

a260713p12.summary: SK Hynix shares tumbled more than 15% in Seoul on Monday to clock their worst day after the chipmaker's strong Nasdaq debut …
a260713p12.title: SK Hynix's stock falls 15%+ in Seoul, its largest-ever decline, after a strong Nasdaq debut, as investors book profits amid its US stock valuation uncertainty (Lee Ying Shan/CNBC)

a260713p13.summary: Chinese President Xi Jinping will make his first appearance at the country's flagship AI conference, signaling …
a260713p13.title: China says President Xi will deliver a keynote speech at the opening ceremony of the three-day World AI Conference in Shanghai on Friday, his first appearance (Bloomberg)

a260713p14.summary: Moscow's Federal Security Service is behind the cyber espionage and sabotage campaigns, EU says.
a260713p14.title: The EU blacklists Russian intelligence group members it says were responsible for spying on and hacking targets across the EU and Ukraine from as early as 2010 (Politico)

a260713p15.summary: Meta Platforms Inc. has committed to spending an additional $40 billion on its sprawling data center campus in Louisiana …
a260713p15.title: Meta says it will spend an extra $40B on its nearly 4,000-acre data center campus in Louisiana, aiming for 5GW+ of compute, pushing its total spend beyond $250B (Riley Griffin/Bloomberg)

a260713p16.summary: Samsung Electronics aims to begin operations at its first chipmaking plant in the city of Yongin, south of Seoul, by 2029 …
a260713p16.title: Samsung says it now aims to begin operations at its first chipmaking plant in South Korea's Yongin by 2029, bringing the timeline forward from 2030 or 2031 (Henry Siu/The Information)

a260713p17.summary: Nearly 200 economists signed a letter calling for policymakers to do more to understand and respond to potential A.I. disruptions.
a260713p17.title: Nearly 200 economists, including 15 Nobel laureates and Anthropic's Jack Clark, sign a letter titled We Must Act Now, warning of rapid AI-led job displacement (Ben Casselman/New York Times)

a260713p18.summary: Move strengthens Dublin's role in Europe's bid to secure advanced semiconductor manufacturing
a260713p18.title: Intel plans to invest €5B to expand chip manufacturing at its Leixlip facility in Ireland; in 2025, Intel canceled a planned €30B factory in Magdeburg, Germany (Financial Times)

a260713p19.summary: The AI talent wars aren't letting up — and neither is Anthropic's big-name hiring spree. — The AI startup has hired Tom Blomfield …
a260713p19.title: Anthropic hires Tom Blomfield, a Monzo co-founder and one of the biggest names in UK tech, to join its compute team; he is taking a leave of absence from YC (Robert Scammell/Business Insider)

a260713p2.summary: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co (TSMC) (2330.TW) will add two advanced chip packaging plants in the Chiayi Science Park …
a260713p2.title: A Taiwanese minister says TSMC plans two more advanced chip packaging plants in the Chiayi Science Park, with all four set to generate $9.35B+ in annual output (Wen-Yee Lee/Reuters)

a260713p20.summary: Apple Inc.'s lawsuit accusing OpenAI of systematically stealing its intellectual property threatens to disrupt …
a260713p20.title: Source: OpenAI still believes it is on track to unveil its first device in 2026 and release it in 2027; Apple's lawsuit may complicate hiring and supply chains (Mark Gurman/Bloomberg)

a260713p21.summary: Anthropic and OpenAI's warnings about “adversarial distillation” are reinvigorating one of the oldest debates in Silicon Valley.
a260713p21.title: Sources: the US estimates unauthorized distillation costs US AI labs up to $6B/year; Silicon Valley told the White House it could become an existential threat (Maggie Eastland/Bloomberg)

a260713p22.summary: Investors are flocking back to Apple Inc. as nervousness about artificial intelligence spending weighs on the stocks of chipmakers and cloud-computing giants.
a260713p22.title: Apple's stock is up 16% since June 25, adding ~$650B in market value and pushing shares back to record territory, as investors flee an AI stock selloff to Apple (Ryan Vlastelica/Bloomberg)

a260713p23.summary: Global smartphone shipments fell 11% YoY in Q2 2026 reaching the lowest second-quarter levels since 2013 as the DRAM and NAND shortage intensified.
a260713p23.title: Global smartphone shipments fell 11% YoY in Q2 to the lowest Q2 levels since 2013 amid the DRAM and NAND shortage; Samsung returns to #1 with a 24% market share (Shilpi Jain/Counterpoint Research)

a260713p3.summary: ADI Predictstreet bungled a World Cup ticket giveaway, users say — Vukašin Petrovi&cacute was elated …
a260713p3.title: A look at ADI Predictstreet's troubled launch, marked by tiny trading volumes, withdrawal bugs, and other issues, as it tried to challenge Kalshi and Polymarket (Wall Street Journal)

a260713p4.summary: Even a chatbot trained on nutrition and fitness research that dispenses reasonable-sounding guidance can become a deadly influence
a260713p4.title: Eating disorder therapists say patients are increasingly using AI chatbots for advice, while AI also appears to be directing more patients to helplines (Julie Jargon/Wall Street Journal)

a260713p5.summary: In at least two places, Uber has pushed a policy that could give it an advantage over developers of self-driving cars.
a260713p5.title: Docs: Uber lobbied for a "phased transition" to AVs, giving it an edge over self-driving developers; Uber says AV industry proposals overlook drivers' rights (Aarian Marshall/Wired)

a260713p6.summary: Helsing has raised a financing round at a valuation of $18 billion from investors including the growth equity arm …
a260713p6.title: Munich-based defense tech startup Helsing raised a $1.8B Series E from Dragoneer, Iconiq, and others at an $18B valuation; Germany made a €270M order this year (Mark Bergen/Bloomberg)

a260713p7.summary: Terry Ellis's fixer wore an overcoat, a multicolored ascot, a Rolex. He had a husky voice and a good suntan.
a260713p7.title: A look back at the 2007 heist of a Verizon data center in London, highlighting the role of physical data security as cyber defenses grow more sophisticated (Nathaniel Rich/New York Times)

a260713p8.summary: Researchers cobbled together funding and time to show how quantum computing could aid in the development of drugs to help underserved populations and combat rare diseases.
a260713p8.title: How a Denmark-based research team is using quantum computers to accelerate AI protein discovery, showing a near-term commercial application for the quantum tech (Isabella Ward/Wired)

a260713p9.summary: The fast-fashion retailer could list in Hong Kong as early as the third quarter of this year — Fast-fashion retailer Shein …
a260713p9.title: Sources: Shein is targeting a $40B+ valuation after winning Beijing's approval for a Hong Kong IPO, set for as early as Q3; Shein canceled its US IPO in 2024 (Wall Street Journal)
